content.full_text:
TIM FARLEY
334 ORCHARDVIEW AVENUE
MARTINEZ, CALIFORNIA 94553
(510) 228-8050


May 18, 1992


Fair Political Practices Committee
Legal Division
428 J Street
Box 807
Sacramento, California 95812

To Whom It May Concern:

Per my conversation with Ani Kindal, I am requesting formal
advice regarding the permissibility of some fundraising.

Background and facts:

I am currently a Legislative Assistant to Assemblymember Robert
Campbell.

In November 1990, I was elected to the Martinez City Council and
am currently in the middle of my term.

On April 26, 1992 I was elected as a delegate to the Democratic
National Convention at the Seventh Congressional District
presidential caucus, pledged to Bill Clinton. I was ranked
number one and will most likely attend the convention this
summer.

Due to the fact that the convention is in New York City, it is my
belief that this trip will be very costly. Therefore I am
exploring the possibilities of raising funds to assist with this
trip. I do not anticipate raising more than $1,200-1,500 for
this event.

My Questions:

1) What are the limitations and implications of raising money
through my City Council Campaign Committee and using these funds
to offset the cost of the trip to New York? These costs include
airfare and hotel.

2) What are the limitations of establishing a separate
controlled committee with the specific intention of offsetting
the cost of this trip, i.e. Committee for Convention
Participation? The money raised would be used exclusively for
the convention and closed after the July meeting.

3) Lastly, what are the limitations of simply raising the money
without benefit of any committee?

Due to the fact that the convention begins eight weeks from
today, I would appreciate a timely response to this request.

Sincerely,

Tim Farley
Tim Farley

and facts:

I am currently a Legislative Assistant to Assemblymember Robert Campbell.

In November 1990, I was elected to the Martinez City Council and am currently in the middle of my term.

On April 26, 1992 I was elected as a delegate to the Democratic National Convention at the Seventh Congressional District presidential caucus, pledged to Bill Clinton. I was ranked number one and will most likely attend the convention this summer.

Due to the fact that the convention is in New York City, it is my belief that this trip will be very costly. Therefore I am exploring the possibilities of raising funds to assist with this trip. I do not anticipate raising more than $1,200-1,500 for this event.

My Questions:

1) What are the limitations and implications of raising money through my City Council Campaign Committee and using these funds to offset the cost of the trip to New York? These costs include airfare and hotel.

2) What are the limitations of establishing a separate controlled committee with the specific intention of offsetting the cost of this trip, i.e. Committee for Convention participation? The money raised would be used exclusively for the convention and closed after the July meeting.

3) Lastly, what are the limitations of simply raising the money without benefit of any committee?

Due to the fact that the convention begins eight weeks from today, I would appreciate a timely response to this request.
